name: Devil's Advocate Validator
description: Receives a SMILE implementation plan from Agent A and critically evaluates it using real case study evidence from the LPI. Queries get_case_studies and get_insights to find where similar implementations succeeded or failed. Outputs a typed JSON critique with risk score, phase-level findings, red flags, and a concrete recommendation. Every finding cites its LPI source. Part of the Level 4 Secure Agent Mesh.

supportedInterfaces.comment: Send plan JSON (from planner.py) to stdin, receive critique JSON on stdout. Requires: npm run build, ollama serve.

inputSchema.description: Must be a valid plan produced by the SMILE Implementation Planner agent.

skills.name: Devil's Advocate Plan Validator
skills.description: Critically evaluates a SMILE implementation plan using LPI case study evidence and scenario insights. Returns risk score (1-10), phase-level critiques with evidence sources, red flags, validated phases, and a concrete recommendation. Designed to be chained after the SMILE Implementation Planner.
examples: Validate a healthcare digital twin plan with a 3-month timeline
Critique a manufacturing predictive maintenance SMILE plan
Challenge assumptions in a smart building energy optimization plan

security.inputValidation: Schema validation on incoming plan — rejects malformed or unexpected payloads before processing.
security.injectionPrevention: String fields from plan are re-sanitized before use in LPI queries.
security.outputSchema: Critique schema validated before returning to orchestrator.
